description_long: This is a tall, narrow-necked ceramic vase with a single curved handle extending from the shoulder to the rim. The body is decorated with black-figure artwork, depicting human figures and possibly mythological scenes, reminiscent of ancient Greek pottery. The surface shows a matte, slightly rough finish with visible wear and some faded areas, suggesting age and historical use. The base and neck are painted black, contrasting with the natural terracotta color of the rest of the vessel. The mouth is wide and circular, suitable for pouring or holding flowers or liquids. The overall form is elegant and functional, with a stable, flared foot for support.
description: A tall, ancient-style ceramic vase with a single handle and black-figure artwork depicting human figures, finished in matte terracotta and black paint.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main decorative scene with multiple human figures and the handle visible on the left.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the handle and a single prominent figure in the artwork, with the rim and base clearly visible.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the plain, undecorated back of the vase and the full curve of the handle.
description_view_3: Angled view displaying the handle and a partial figure, with more of the vase's surface wear visible.
